data.site_tagline: A SaaS starter that looks ready for launch, not ready for redesign.

data.title: Pricing, integrations, and changelog storytelling from the first deploy.
data.subtitle: This starter gives product teams narrative marketing sections without sacrificing maintainable Nuxt structure.

data.meta_description: A premium Nuxt SaaS starter with pricing, integrations, and Contentrain-ready content models.

data.blurb: A stronger SaaS starting point for product marketing teams that care about rhythm, proof, and clarity.

data.title: Narrative product marketing
data.summary: Guide buyers from context to proof to pricing without flattening every section into the same layout.

data.title: Shared content contracts
data.summary: Every important marketing surface maps cleanly to a Contentrain model instead of scattered page copy.

data.title: Nuxt-native structure
data.summary: Keep composables, pages, and SEO in the patterns Nuxt teams already expect to maintain.

data.name: Starter
data.summary: For small launches that need a stronger first impression.

data.name: Scale
data.summary: For SaaS teams shipping pricing, integrations, and changelog storytelling.

data.name: Studio
data.summary: For product suites that need a fuller content and release narrative.

data.name: Stripe
data.summary: Payments, subscription flows, and monetization events.

data.name: Supabase
data.summary: Modern database, auth, and edge function workflows.

data.name: PostHog
data.summary: Product analytics, experiments, and event understanding.

data.name: HubSpot
data.summary: CRM, lifecycle messaging, and sales handoff.

data.name: Linear
data.summary: Issue planning and changelog-linked product execution.

data.quote: It already feels like a product company site, not a UI kit demo.
The pricing and integration surfaces are opinionated in the right places.

data.question: Why Nuxt for a SaaS starter?
data.answer: Nuxt gives a strong page architecture, ergonomic server routes, and native patterns for multi-page product marketing.

data.question: How does Contentrain fit into the app?
data.answer: Pricing tiers, integrations, testimonials, release notes, and global site settings are all modeled as content and exposed through the generated SDK.

data.title: Multi-surface launch copy
data.summary: Added campaign, pricing, and proof surfaces that can be operated as content.

entries.body:
This release connected product marketing sections to a shared content model.

Teams can now evolve hero messaging, pricing details, and proof modules without reworking page structure.

data.title: Deploy-ready SEO and metadata
data.summary: Improved metadata defaults and starter deployment posture.

entries.body:
Metadata, canonical settings, and starter-level content defaults now ship with the project.

That makes the first deployment look intentional instead of incomplete.
